briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 11, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Domestic and International Realities
sections.content: Domestically, the combined effects of large-scale wildfires and climate change have led to a shortage of fruit tree seedlings, directly impacting farms [1]. The economic burden of climate change is becoming tangible, with an estimated global damage of 280,000 KRW per ton of greenhouse gas [4]. Internationally, Iran's water shortage crisis is intensifying due to the dual challenges of climate change and war [5]. Global warming is accelerating, and changes in natural ecosystems are evident, such as the earlier arrival of spring [59, 62]. In Canada, climate change and wildfire smoke have emerged as major disaster concerns [71]. Furthermore, factors hindering climate action coexist globally, including policy reversals such as budget cuts to the Department of Environment and Climate Change threatening Arctic science [77] and the Australian government's fossil fuel subsidies [81], alongside a four-year consecutive decline in global climate change news coverage [66], raising serious concerns.

sections.title: 🌱 Corporate and National Efforts and Achievements in Climate Change Response
sections.content: Major domestic companies are actively responding to climate change. SK Hynix has been inducted into the CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) Climate Change 'Hall of Fame' for 13 consecutive years, setting the longest domestic record, and also achieved a double crown in water management [8, 10, 13, 16, 19, 20, 23]. Hyundai Engineering & Construction was also selected for 'Top Rating' and the 'Hall of Fame' in the CDP Climate Change sector for eight consecutive years [7, 12, 39]. KT&G, Hanwha, and Hyundai Glovis were also recognized as excellent companies in the CDP Climate Change and Water Management sectors [3, 11, 14, 18, 21]. Meanwhile, the Republic of Korea signed an MOU with Ghana to strengthen cooperation in joint climate change response [6, 9, 15, 17, 24], and Da Nang City in Vietnam is also strengthening cooperation with Canada in climate change adaptation, indicating ongoing international collaboration [22]. These efforts are positive signals demonstrating the commitment of companies and nations to address the climate crisis.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Carbon Neutrality Transition and Climate Technology Innovation
sections.content: Domestic and international efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are accelerating in various forms. Related discussions are active, with events such as the 10th Carbon Neutral Energy Transition Forum being held [26]. Campaigns like tree planting for carbon neutrality in daily life [27] and Gyeryong City's Carbon Neutrality Point system, which offers up to 100,000 KRW for reducing driving mileage [31, 33, 35, 40], are gaining traction [34]. Notably, climate technology innovation is drawing attention, with 'WATER KOREA 2026,' where AI and carbon-neutral water technologies converge, opening in Busan [28]. The Korea Environment Corporation has also embarked on developing an AI-based carbon emission management system through public-private cooperation [37, 38]. Gyeonggi Province is seeking 10 promising climate tech companies to lead carbon neutrality [32], and the UK construction industry has announced a new 'Net Zero Carbon Building Standard' [80], indicating continued carbon neutrality initiatives combining technology and policy.

sections.title: ⚠️ Opportunities and Challenges in Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is central to addressing climate change but simultaneously faces various challenges. Positive developments include increased interest in renewable energy investment due to rising power demand from AI data centers [42], expanded investment in offshore wind and solar power by the financial sector [49], and Jeonnam Province's creation of public-led power generation complexes [44]. In Gyeonggi Province's industrial complexes, solar power generation increased by 260 MW in three years [51]. Regional efforts are also active, such as expanding cooperation for testing and certification of new and renewable energy facilities and establishing citizen funds [43, 45, 50, 55]. However, numerous challenges remain, including political movements asserting 'climate change is a hoax' [2], the encroachment of Chinese products into the solar power market [46], and Chungbuk Province's low electricity self-sufficiency rate [47]. Furthermore, instability is emerging even in regions once dubbed 'renewable energy kingdoms' [41]. Calls for policy improvements are also growing, advocating for redesigning new and renewable energy policies to prioritize community benefits [52] and rationally easing regulations on solar panel setback distances [48].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights the severity of the climate crisis, alongside the convergence of diverse domestic and international efforts to overcome it. For a sustainable future, practical actions based on scientific facts and robust policy support are more crucial than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Hyundai Motor Ranks Global Top 2 in Operating Profit, Surpassing Volkswagen for the First Time
articles.original_title: 현대차, 글로벌 영업익 ‘톱2’…‘판매 2위’ 폭스바겐 첫 추월
articles.description: Despite ranking 3rd in sales volume, performance reached 20 trillion KRW. Local production defended against tariff shocks. Success of mid-to-large SUVs and Genesis models. 'Middle East uncertainties to be overcome through diversification.' In a volatile automotive market, shaken by US tariff policies and the rise of Chinese electric vehicles, Hyundai Motor Group's operating profit (around 20 trillion KRW) surpassed Volkswagen Group (around 15 trillion KRW) for the first time last year, ranking second globally. This year, despite various geopolitical uncertainties such as the Middle East situation, consumer...

summary.phenomenon: Last year, Hyundai Motor Group's operating profit reached approximately 20 trillion KRW, surpassing Volkswagen Group (around 15 trillion KRW) for the first time, securing the second position in global operating profit.
summary.cause: Hyundai Motor Group mitigated the impact of US tariffs through local production, and the success of its mid-to-large SUVs and Genesis models contributed to its performance.
summary.outlook: Although geopolitical uncertainties, such as the situation in the Middle East, are anticipated this year, Hyundai Motor Group is expected to overcome these challenges through a diversification strategy.

articles.title: Peak interest: Toronto’s snow mountains that refuse to melt are a toxic hazard
articles.description: Reaching up to 100ft, these massive piles contain tonnes of salt that keep roads clear – but pose environmental risksMost mountains take tens of millions of years to form. Toronto’s newest mountain took just days.Towering atop the crowns of evergreens, it has no skeleton of limestone or granite. There are no spires, cornices or headwalls. It is simply piles upon piles of snow, mixed with a toxic cocktail of road salt, antifreeze, oil, coffee cups and lost keys. It is the final resting place for

summary.phenomenon: Massive snow piles, reaching up to 100 feet (approximately 30 meters) in height, have formed in Toronto, Canada, within days and are not melting.
summary.cause: These snow piles are composed of snow mixed with tons of salt used for road clearing, along with hazardous substances such as antifreeze, oil, coffee cups, and lost keys.
summary.outlook: These snow piles pose environmental risks.
